title: The One with the Lesbian Wedding

jokes.text: There was a projectile, uh...throwing up incident, but he started it.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining how things went with the baby
jokes.punchline: Blaming the baby for starting the vomiting incident
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's awkwardness with babies, economical phrasing with unexpected blame assignment

jokes.text: We're getting married.
jokes.setup: Carol building up to announce something important about 'us'
jokes.punchline: The awkward bombshell announcement to Ross
jokes.explanation: Creates tension and sets up the episode's central conflict, well-timed reveal

jokes.text: As in, 'I now pronounce you wife and wife' married?
jokes.setup: Ross processing the marriage announcement
jokes.punchline: Clever wordplay on traditional wedding vows adjusted for lesbian couple
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay that addresses the situation directly with humor, very quotable

jokes.text: Why wouldn't I want to come? I had fun at the first wedding.
jokes.setup: Carol asking if Ross would want to attend their wedding
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically referencing his own wedding to Carol that ended in divorce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that could only come from Ross in this situation, perfectly bitter and self-aware

jokes.text: What would it say about me if I couldn't, uh, revel in your joy? And I'm reveling, baby. Believe me.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to convince himself and others he's happy about the wedding
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top fake enthusiasm with 'reveling, baby'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ross's desperate attempt to appear mature and supportive

jokes.text: Is your finger caught in that chair? Mm-hm. Want us to go? Uh-huh.
jokes.setup: Ross's emotional pretense about being happy for them
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy reveal that he's literally trapped and needs them to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline to Ross's emotional struggle, very economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Then I started thinking about us, and how these are the days of our lives.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about being on Days of Our Lives
jokes.punchline: Using the soap opera title to describe his real life with pretentious earnestness
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey earnestness about something silly, perfect character integration

jokes.text: [IN UNISON] Yes!
jokes.setup: Carol and Susan's caterer had an accident and is in a full-body cast
jokes.punchline: Monica and Rachel's inappropriately enthusiastic reaction to someone's injury
jokes.explanation: Good timing and character work showing their selfish excitement about Monica getting the catering job

jokes.text: They love each other. They wanna celebrate that love with the people close to them. If you wanna call that a reason.
jokes.setup: Ross questioning why Carol and Susan need to get married
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic dismissal of love as a 'reason' for marriage
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery that undercuts Ross's cynicism with logic

jokes.text: [SINGING] Who's the bitterest man In the living room? The bitterest man In the living room? Hi, neighbor.
jokes.setup: Ross being bitter about the wedding situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler singing a mocking song about Ross's bitterness
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler mockery with musical element, very quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: If she were marrying a guy, she'd be the worst lesbian ever.
jokes.setup: Ross saying if Carol were marrying a guy he wouldn't be expected to attend
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out the obvious logical flaw in Ross's argument
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical humor that completely deflates Ross's argument with perfect economy

jokes.text: No, I'm on right after this guy shoots himself.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if they missed Joey's scene
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning suicide as a TV timing cue
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered with Joey's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: She taught me about how to work with the cameras and smell-the-fart acting.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about what another actress taught him
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing or misunderstanding 'sfumato' or similar acting technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey malapropism that became iconic, could only come from his character

jokes.text: Like this: [pause for intense look]
jokes.setup: Joey explaining 'smell-the-fart acting'
jokes.punchline: Visual demonstration of the ridiculous acting technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual payoff to the previous joke, shows rather than tells

jokes.text: Perhaps we can discuss this further over coffee.
jokes.setup: Joey as Dr. Drake Ramoray delivering serious medical news
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately hitting on the patient's sister during medical crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's sleazy soap opera character

jokes.text: For a minute, I thought you were actually smelling something.
jokes.setup: Joey demonstrating his 'smell-the-fart acting' during his soap scene
jokes.punchline: Chandler commenting on Joey's intense facial expression
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the previous joke with Chandler's observational style

jokes.text: I gotta get to work. I got a big dinosaur bone to inspect. No, that's me. Oh, right.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he has a big dinosaur bone to inspect
jokes.punchline: Chandler's innuendo interpretation and Ross's innocent correction
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke that works for the characters but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: One of my clients died on the massage table today.
jokes.setup: Phoebe looking upset and being asked what's wrong
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of shocking news about client death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect Phoebe matter-of-factness, very economical setup

jokes.text: That's a little more relaxed than you want them to get.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her client died during massage
jokes.punchline: Chandler's darkly comic understatement about death during relaxation
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor with classic Chandler deadpan delivery, very quotable

jokes.text: Little did she know God thought, 'Okay, but that's it.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing how Mrs. Adelman probably planned her day
jokes.punchline: Imagining God casually limiting her activities to death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that's perfectly Phoebe - spiritual but irreverent, great structure

jokes.text: I can't decide between lamb or duck. Well, lambs are scarier. Or the movie would've been called Silence of the Ducks.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to decide between lamb or duck for catering
jokes.punchline: Chandler's reference to 'Silence of the Lambs' with duck substitution
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference joke with wordplay, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I had the half-drunk cappuccino with the lipstick on the rim. And this with the cigarette butt in it, is that decaf?
jokes.setup: Characters at a messy restaurant trying to identify their orders
jokes.punchline: Rachel describing disgusting details of dirty cups as if they're menu options
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about restaurant service, but not character-specific

jokes.text: [AS MRS. ADELMAN] Talk about crap, just listen to Stella Neidman tell her story about Rod Steiger for the hundredth time.
jokes.setup: Phoebe being possessed by elderly woman's spirit
jokes.punchline: Old lady complaints delivered through Phoebe's voice
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Phoebe's possession gimmick, authentic old person complaints

jokes.text: [AS MRS. ADELMAN] Sit up!
jokes.setup: Phoebe channeling the spirit of an elderly woman
jokes.punchline: Sudden authoritative outburst in elderly woman's voice
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment, economical and fits the possession bit

jokes.text: Is it a living room? Is it a restaurant? Who can tell? I guess that's the fun.
jokes.setup: Rachel's mom commenting on Central Perk's ambiguous decor
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic agreement about the 'fun' of confusion
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about coffee shop design, serviceable but not memorable

jokes.text: The last time I saw you, it was eat or be eaten.
jokes.setup: Sandra greeting Monica and commenting on her appearance
jokes.punchline: Reference to Monica's past eating disorder in dramatic terms
jokes.explanation: Callback to Monica's history but somewhat insensitive, mixed success

jokes.text: If you didn't pour the coffee, no one would have anything to drink. Believe me, sometimes that happens.
jokes.setup: Sandra trying to make Rachel's waitressing job sound important
jokes.punchline: Rachel's deadpan admission that she sometimes fails at basic job duties
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that fits Rachel's character development

jokes.text: [AS MRS. ADELMAN] I know who you remind me of. Evelyn Durmer. That's before the lousy face-lift. Now she looks like Soupy Sales.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Adelman's spirit commenting on Sandra
jokes.punchline: Catty old lady observations about plastic surgery and dated references
jokes.explanation: Authentic elderly woman cattiness but relies on dated references

jokes.text: There's an unattractive nude man playing the cello. Just be glad he's not playing a smaller instrument.
jokes.setup: Sandra commenting on nude cello player they can see
jokes.punchline: Monica's innuendo about instrument size correlation
jokes.explanation: Clever visual/innuendo combination with good structure and timing

jokes.text: I'm, uh, considering leaving your father.
jokes.setup: Sandra and Rachel having a heart-to-heart about Rachel's happiness
jokes.punchline: Bombshell announcement that completely shifts the conversation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic timing that creates major plot development and comedy through discomfort

jokes.text: Lesbian wedding, chicken breasts. Oh, God. I think I'm gonna be sick. What? It's not like I'm putting little nipples on them.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if lesbian wedding/chicken breast combination is too cute
jokes.punchline: Escalating to unnecessary nipple detail when defending the choice
jokes.explanation: Good Monica perfectionism/overthinking moment with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: When I was little, all parents got divorced. I figured as an adult, I wouldn't have to worry about it.
jokes.setup: Rachel processing her parents' potential divorce
jokes.punchline: Ironic observation about timing of when divorce concerns should end
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about generational expectations and timing

jokes.text: When my parents divorced, they sent me to a shrink and she told me that kids blame themselves. But in your case, it's actually kind of true.
jokes.setup: Ross offering comfort about parents blaming themselves for divorce
jokes.punchline: Completely undermining the comfort by saying Rachel actually is to blame
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected comfort - starts supportive then delivers devastating punchline

jokes.text: Damn. My mail-order grandfather hasn't come yet.
jokes.setup: Mr. Adelman arriving at the door
jokes.punchline: Chandler's absurd comment about ordering a grandfather through mail
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Chandler humor with unexpected concept

jokes.text: Although you did cut into my busy day of sitting.
jokes.setup: Thanking Mr. Adelman for coming to meet Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating humor about having nothing to do as elderly person
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware elderly humor with nice deadpan delivery

jokes.text: No, I spent most of my morning trying to stand up.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he wants to sit down
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of elderly mobility struggles
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly person self-deprecation, functional but predictable

jokes.text: I remember she also said she wanted to sleep with me one last time. I'm sorry. There's laughing in my head. Worth a shot, huh?
jokes.setup: Mr. Adelman explaining his wife's unfinished business
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual request to possessed Phoebe, followed by his casual acceptance of rejection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with elderly man's matter-of-fact approach to inappropriate request

jokes.text: See, he's actually an orthodontist, but he came as a regular dentist.
jokes.setup: Looking at Halloween photos and explaining Barry's costume
jokes.punchline: The absurdly subtle difference in his professional costume choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that shows Barry's lack of imagination and creativity

jokes.text: Actually, I think you said: 'Don't touch that and get out of my kitchen.' Really? Weird. Anyway...
jokes.setup: Monica claiming she said she doesn't need help
jokes.punchline: Joey's accurate memory of her much ruder actual words and Monica's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good Monica character work showing her selective memory about being controlling

jokes.text: Do you know what we should do? Does anybody have any marijuana?
jokes.setup: Sandra excited about 'girls time' and suggesting activities
jokes.punchline: Conservative mother casually asking for illegal drugs
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - uptight mother trying to be cool in awkward way

jokes.text: What's new in sex?
jokes.setup: Sandra trying to have modern girl talk with the group
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately direct question about sex from conservative mother
jokes.explanation: Very economical cringe humor showing Sandra's awkward attempt at modern conversation

jokes.text: I'm dicing. I'm dicing. I don't hear anything.
jokes.setup: Sandra asking about sex and mentioning only being with Rachel's father
jokes.punchline: Monica desperately trying to avoid hearing intimate details about Rachel's parents
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Monica's discomfort with inappropriate conversation

jokes.text: You didn't marry your Barry, honey. But I married mine.
jokes.setup: Sandra explaining why Rachel should understand her wanting to leave
jokes.punchline: Parallel between Rachel not marrying Barry and Sandra regretting marrying hers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel structure that reframes the pilot episode's central conflict

jokes.text: I feel like you should have German subtitles.
jokes.setup: Monica being extremely militaristic about cooking preparation
jokes.punchline: Chandler comparing her to a Nazi/German war movie
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor for Monica's controlling kitchen behavior

jokes.text: I'm sorry! It's the pigs! They're reluctant to get in the blanket!
jokes.setup: Monica yelling at Joey to speed up food preparation
jokes.punchline: Joey anthropomorphizing the pigs-in-a-blanket as uncooperative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey absurdism - treating food as having personality and agency

jokes.text: But if you don't help, I'm gonna take those hot dogs and create an appetizer called 'Pigs In Ross.'
jokes.setup: Ross refusing to help with wedding preparation
jokes.punchline: Monica threatening violence with pun on 'pigs in a blanket'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay threat that shows Monica's stress and determination

jokes.text: You're still gonna pay me, right? Or something a little less selfish.
jokes.setup: Carol saying they might call off the wedding
jokes.punchline: Monica's immediate selfish concern followed by self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good Monica moment showing her money concerns and immediate self-correction

jokes.text: If my parents didn't want me to marry you...no way that would've stopped me.
jokes.setup: Ross encouraging Carol to go ahead with the wedding despite her parents
jokes.punchline: Ironic statement given that his marriage to Carol ended in divorce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Ross giving marriage advice about a marriage that failed

jokes.text: I feel like Superman without my powers, you know? I have the cape, and yet I cannot fly.
jokes.setup: Joey lamenting his lack of success with women at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Superman metaphor for romantic frustration
jokes.explanation: Good Joey melodrama with extended metaphor that fits his dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: Well, now you understand how I feel every single day. The world is my lesbian wedding.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about feeling powerless with women
jokes.punchline: Chandler relating it to his own dating struggles with metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures Chandler's dating struggles with memorable phrasing

jokes.text: [AS MRS. ADELMAN] Butterscotch? No one? All right, you'll be sorry later.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony beginning with romantic atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Elderly spirit offering candy and making predictions about regret
jokes.explanation: Typical elderly person behavior that fits the character but isn't particularly funny

jokes.text: [AS MRS. ADELMAN] Oh, my God. Now I've seen everything! [IN NORMAL VOICE] Whoa, she's gone.
jokes.setup: The wedding ceremony proceeding with two women getting married
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Adelman's spirit finally satisfied after seeing lesbian wedding, then departing
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the possession storyline with logical resolution and timing

jokes.text: How's that pig-in-the-blanket working out for you? I wrapped those bad boys.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation at the wedding reception
jokes.punchline: Joey's pride in his food preparation work with casual slang
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to earlier cooking chaos with Joey's characteristic pride in simple tasks

jokes.text: I think it's time you forget about Rose, move on with your life. How about we get you a drink?
jokes.setup: Mr. Adelman talking about missing his dead wife Rose
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt advice to move on followed by immediate alcohol suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler inappropriate advice with alcohol as solution

jokes.text: I just danced with a wonderfully large woman. And three other girls made eyes at me over at the buffet.
jokes.setup: Sandra enjoying herself at the lesbian wedding
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic description of romantic attention from women with diplomatic language
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Sandra's awakening sexuality with polite language

jokes.text: Look, penis, schmenis! Okay? We're all people.
jokes.setup: Ross at the lesbian wedding, having evolved his perspective
jokes.punchline: Dismissive Yiddish-influenced phrase about male anatomy importance
jokes.explanation: Great character growth moment with memorable phrasing showing Ross's acceptance

tmdb_overview: Monica caters her ex-sister-in-law's wedding. Phoebe experiences a possessive spirit; Rachel's mother reveals a bombshell.
